devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अथ कृष्णः परिवृतो ज्ञातिभिर्मुदितात्मभिः । अनुगीयमानो न्यविशद्व्रजं गोकुलमण्डितम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaatha kṛṣṇaḥ parivṛto jñātibhirmuditātmabhiḥanugīyamāno nyaviśadvrajaṃ gokulamaṇḍitam
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Surrounded by his kinsmen whose hearts were full of delight, Kṛṣṇa, whose glories were sung (by them), entered Vraja (looking) beautiful with herds of cows.

devanagari: व्रजे विक्रीडतोरेवं गोपालच्छद्ममायया । ग्रीष्मो नामर्तुरभवन्नातिप्रेयाञ्छरीरिणाम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: vraje vikrīḍatorevaṃ gopālacchadmamāyayāgrīṣmo nāmarturabhavannātipreyāñcharīriṇām
translation: While they (Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa) assuming through their Māyā power, the guise of cowherds, were indulging in various sports, there was the advent of the season called the Summer which is not much pleasant to embodied beings.

devanagari: स च वृन्दावनगुणैर्वसन्त इव लक्षितः । यत्रास्ते भगवान्साक्षाद्रा मेण सह केशवः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ca vṛndāvanaguṇairvasanta iva lakṣitaḥyatrāste bhagavānsākṣādrā meṇa saha keśavaḥ
translation: It, however, appeared like the spring due to the special (environmental) characteristics of Vṛndāvana where the glorious Lord Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) was personally staying along with Balarāma.

devanagari: यत्र निर्झरनिर्ह्राद निवृत्तस्वनझिल्लिकम् । शश्वत्तच्छीकरर्जीष द्रुममण्डलमण्डितम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra nirjharanirhrāda nivṛttasvanajhillikamśaśvattacchīkararjīṣa drumamaṇḍalamaṇḍitam
translation: It resounded with the sound of cascades that drowned the jingling of the crickets. It looked beautiful with clusters of trees constantly sprayed with the particles of water from the waterfalls.

devanagari: सरित्सरःप्रस्रवणोर्मिवायुना कह्लारकञ्जोत्पलरेणुहारिणा । न विद्यते यत्र वनौकसां दवो निदाघवह्न्यर्कभवोऽतिशाद्वले ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: saritsaraḥprasravaṇormivāyunā kahlārakañjotpalareṇuhāriṇāna vidyate yatra vanaukasāṃ davo nidāghavahnyarkabhavo'tiśādvale
translation: Due to the pleasant breeze blowing over the waves of the rivers and the pools (and the cascades) and moistened with spray and carrying the fragrant pollen of white lilies, blue lotuses and other flowers, the inhabitants of that forest (Vṛndāvana) which, covered with meadows richly overgrown with green grass, did not experience the scorching heat radiating from the sun and forest fires in the summer.

devanagari: अगाधतोयह्रदिनीतटोर्मिभिर्द्रवत्पुरीष्याः पुलिनैः समन्ततः । न यत्र चण्डांशुकरा विषोल्बणा भुवो रसं शाद्वलितं च गृह्णते ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: agādhatoyahradinītaṭormibhirdravatpurīṣyāḥ pulinaiḥ samantataḥna yatra caṇḍāṃśukarā viṣolbaṇā bhuvo rasaṃ śādvalitaṃ ca gṛhṇate
translation: At the place, the scorching rays of the sun, fierce like virulent poison, were not able to dry up the moisture and verdant nature of the soil wherein the mixture of sand and mire on all sides of the bank remained always wet by the waves that used to beat against the banks of rivers with unfathomable water.

devanagari: वनं कुसुमितं श्रीमन्नदच्चित्रमृगद्विजम् । गायन्मयूरभ्रमरं कूजत्कोकिलसारसम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: vanaṃ kusumitaṃ śrīmannadaccitramṛgadvijamgāyanmayūrabhramaraṃ kūjatkokilasārasam
translation: The resplendently beautiful forest was in full blossom and was resounded with the cries and notes of various birds and beasts, with singing peacocks and humming bees and warbling cuckoos and waterfowls.

devanagari: क्रीडिष्यमाणस्तत्क्र्ष्णो भगवान्बलसंयुतः । वेणुं विरणयन्गोपैर्गोधनैः संवृतोऽविशत् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: krīḍiṣyamāṇastatkrṣṇo bhagavānbalasaṃyutaḥveṇuṃ viraṇayangopairgodhanaiḥ saṃvṛto'viśat
translation: With a desire to enjoy sports, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, accompanied by Balarāma and surrounded with cows and cowherds, entered the forest playing upon his flute.

devanagari: प्रवालबर्हस्तबक स्रग्धातुकृतभूषणाः । रामकृष्णादयो गोपा ननृतुर्युयुधुर्जगुः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: pravālabarhastabaka sragdhātukṛtabhūṣaṇāḥrāmakṛṣṇādayo gopā nanṛturyuyudhurjaguḥ
translation: With their persons decorated with tender foliage, peacock feathers, clusters of flowers and coloured earth, Balarāma, Kṛṣṇa and other cowherds indulged in dancing, wrestling and singing.

devanagari: कृष्णस्य नृत्यतः केचिज्जगुः केचिदवादयन् । वेणुपाणितलैः शृङ्गैः प्रशशंसुरथापरे ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇasya nṛtyataḥ kecijjaguḥ kecidavādayanveṇupāṇitalaiḥ śṛṅgaiḥ praśaśaṃsurathāpare
translation: While Kṛṣṇa was dancing, some cowherds sang, some clapped their hands, some played on flutes or blew their horns, while others applauded and cheered up.

devanagari: गोपजातिप्रतिच्छन्ना देवा गोपालरूपिणौ । ईडिरे कृष्णरामौ च नटा इव नटं नृप ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: gopajātipraticchannā devā gopālarūpiṇauīḍire kṛṣṇarāmau ca naṭā iva naṭaṃ nṛpa
translation: Gods who had assumed the forms of cowherds[1] and thus completely concealed their god-head, eulogised in applause both Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, as actors do to cheer up another actor (in spite of their master-servant relation).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā reads gopa-jāti-praticchannau as adj. qualifying ‘Kṛṣṇa-Rāmau’ and interprets: ‘Gods who assumed the forms of cowherds glorified Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma who had completely concealed their divinity in the guise of cowherds.’

devanagari: भ्रमणैर्लङ्घनैः क्षेपैरास्फोटनविकर्षणैः । चिक्रीडतुर्नियुद्धेन काकपक्षधरौ क्वचित् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: bhramaṇairlaṅghanaiḥ kṣepairāsphoṭanavikarṣaṇaiḥcikrīḍaturniyuddhena kākapakṣadharau kvacit
translation: The two brothers wearing side-locks, sometimes, sported by running in circles, long-jumps, shot-putting, striking of arms, tug-of-war and wrestling.

devanagari: क्वचिन्नृत्यत्सु चान्येषु गायकौ वादकौ स्वयम् । शशंसतुर्महाराज साधु साध्विति वादिनौ ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacinnṛtyatsu cānyeṣu gāyakau vādakau svayamśaśaṃsaturmahārāja sādhu sādhviti vādinau
translation: Sometimes while other cowherds were dancing both (Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) sang personally, and played on instruments and applauded (with words) “well-done! Buck up”, Oh great king.

devanagari: क्वचिद्बिल्वैः क्वचित्कुम्भैः क्वचामलकमुष्टिभिः । अस्पृश्यनेत्रबन्धाद्यैः क्वचिन्मृगखगेहया ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacidbilvaiḥ kvacitkumbhaiḥ kvacāmalakamuṣṭibhiḥaspṛśyanetrabandhādyaiḥ kvacinmṛgakhagehayā
translation: Some time they would play with a Bilva fruit, sometime with Kumbha fruit, and now with a handful of Āmalaka fruits (myrobalans). Sometimes (in a running race or with swift movements in an arena) they would playfully not allow themselves to be touched, while at times they would play hide- and-seek after shutting the eyes, of a cowherd boy—or by blindfolding and such other games. Sometimes they would divert themselves by mimicking the sounds and notes of beasts, birds. Now they would indulge in jumping like frogs, and now they would cut jokes of various kinds; now by swinging with the help of branches of trees and sometimes by playing the role of kings.

devanagari: क्वचिच्च दर्दुरप्लावैर्विविधैरुपहासकैः । कदाचित्स्यन्दोलिकया कर्हिचिन्नृपचेष्टया ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacicca darduraplāvairvividhairupahāsakaiḥkadācitsyandolikayā karhicinnṛpaceṣṭayā
translation: Some time they would play with a Bilva fruit, sometime with Kumbha fruit, and now with a handful of Āmalaka fruits (myrobalans). Sometimes (in a running race or with swift movements in an arena) they would playfully not allow themselves to be touched, while at times they would play hide- and-seek after shutting the eyes, of a cowherd boy—or by blindfolding and such other games. Sometimes they would divert themselves by mimicking the sounds and notes of beasts, birds. Now they would indulge in jumping like frogs, and now they would cut jokes of various kinds; now by swinging with the help of branches of trees and sometimes by playing the role of kings.

devanagari: एवं तौ लोकसिद्धाभिः क्रीडाभिश्चेरतुर्वने । नद्यद्रि द्रोणिकुञ्जेषु काननेषु सरःसु च ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ tau lokasiddhābhiḥ krīḍābhiśceraturvanenadyadri droṇikuñjeṣu kānaneṣu saraḥsu ca
translation:
Some time they would play with a Bilva fruit, sometime with Kumbha fruit, and now with a handful of Āmalaka fruits (myrobalans). Sometimes (in a running race or with swift movements in an arena) they would playfully not allow themselves to be touched, while at times they would play hide- and-seek after shutting the eyes, of a cowherd boy—or by blindfolding and such other games. Sometimes they would divert themselves by mimicking the sounds and notes of beasts, birds. Now they would indulge in jumping like frogs, and now they would cut jokes of various kinds; now by swinging with the help of branches of trees and sometimes by playing the role of kings.
Indulging in such sports well known among the people, they rambled all over the area in the rivers, on the mountains, in the valleys, bowers, in woods and lakes.

devanagari: पशूंश्चारयतोर्गोपैस्तद्वने रामकृष्णयोः । गोपरूपी प्रलम्बोऽगादसुरस्तज्जिहीर्षया ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: paśūṃścārayatorgopaistadvane rāmakṛṣṇayoḥgoparūpī pralambo'gādasurastajjihīrṣayā
translation: While Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa were grazing their cattle in the meadows of that forest, a demon called Pralamba assumed the guise of a cowherd and entered his followers with a view to kidnapping them.

devanagari: अथाष्टादशोऽध्यायः 10. ॥ 18 ॥
english_devnagari: athāṣṭādaśo'dhyāyaḥ 10 .
translation: Even though Kṛṣṇa, the omniscient (All-perceiving) Lord of the Yadu clan, knew it (his real intention), he agreed to be his playmate and friend, with an intention of finishing with him.

devanagari: तत्रोपाहूय गोपालान्कृष्णः प्राह विहारवित् । हे गोपा विहरिष्यामो द्वन्द्वीभूय यथायथम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatropāhūya gopālānkṛṣṇaḥ prāha vihāravithe gopā vihariṣyāmo dvandvībhūya yathāyatham
translation: Kṛṣṇa, a past-master in games, summoned all the cowherds and proposed, “Oh comrade-cowherds! Now let us play by dividing ourselves into two suitable parties.”

devanagari: तत्र चक्रुः परिवृढौ गोपा रामजनार्दनौ । कृष्णसङ्घट्टिनः केचिदासन्रामस्य चापरे ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tatra cakruḥ parivṛḍhau gopā rāmajanārdanaukṛṣṇasaṅghaṭṭinaḥ kecidāsanrāmasya cāpare
translation: In that game, the cowherds selected Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa as their captains—some (one party) accepted Kṛṣṇa’s side, while the rest (the other party) Balarāma’s side.

devanagari: आचेरुर्विविधाः क्रीडा वाह्यवाहकलक्षणाः । यत्रारोहन्ति जेतारो वहन्ति च पराजिताः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ācerurvividhāḥ krīḍā vāhyavāhakalakṣaṇāḥyatrārohanti jetāro vahanti ca parājitāḥ
translation: They indulged in various sports characterised by one party members to carry as mounts the members of the other party—the winners being the riders and the defeated partymembers, the carriers.

devanagari: वहन्तो वाह्यमानाश्च चारयन्तश्च गोधनम् । भाण्डीरकं नाम वटं जग्मुः कृष्णपुरोगमाः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: vahanto vāhyamānāśca cārayantaśca godhanambhāṇḍīrakaṃ nāma vaṭaṃ jagmuḥ kṛṣṇapurogamāḥ
translation: In this way, some boys riding and some carrying and simultaneously grazing the cattle—they went near a banyan tree called Bhāṇḍīraka.

devanagari: रामसङ्घट्टिनो यर्हि श्रीदामवृषभादयः । क्रीडायां जयिनस्तांस्तानूहुः कृष्णादयो नृप ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: rāmasaṅghaṭṭino yarhi śrīdāmavṛṣabhādayaḥkrīḍāyāṃ jayinastāṃstānūhuḥ kṛṣṇādayo nṛpa
translation: When cowherds like Śrīdāmā, Vṛṣabha and others belonging to the party of Rāma, became victorious in the game, Kṛṣṇa and others carried them on their backs, Oh King.

devanagari: उवाह कृष्णो भगवान्श्रीदामानं पराजितः । वृषभं भद्रसेनस्तु प्रलम्बो रोहिणीसुतम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: uvāha kṛṣṇo bhagavānśrīdāmānaṃ parājitaḥvṛṣabhaṃ bhadrasenastu pralambo rohiṇīsutam
translation: Being defeated, Lord Kṛṣṇa bore Śrīdāman, Bhadra carried Vṛṣabha and Pralamba took Balarāma, the son of Rohiṇī on his back.

devanagari: अविषह्यं मन्यमानः कृष्णं दानवपुङ्गवः । वहन् द्रुततरं प्रागादवरोहणतः परम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: aviṣahyaṃ manyamānaḥ kṛṣṇaṃ dānavapuṅgavaḥvahan drutataraṃ prāgādavarohaṇataḥ param
translation: Considering that Kṛṣṇa was irresistible, the prominent demon Pralamba swiftly carried away Balarāma far beyond the limit for dismounting.

devanagari: तमुद्वहन्धरणिधरेन्द्र गौरवं । महासुरो विगतरयो निजं वपुः । स आस्थितः पुरटपरिच्छदो बभौ । तडिद्द्युमानुडुपतिवाडिवाम्बुदः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tamudvahandharaṇidharendra gauravaṃmahāsuro vigatarayo nijaṃ vapuḥsa āsthitaḥ puraṭaparicchado babhautaḍiddyumānuḍupativāḍivāmbudaḥ
translation: When his progress was stopped by carrying Balarāma who grew weighty as the mount Meru (the king of all mountains), the demon who assumed his original (demonic) form adorned with gold ornaments and clothes, looked like a cloud illuminated with flashes of lightning carrying the Moon (the Lord of Stars) on his back.

devanagari: निरीक्ष्य तद्वपुरलमम्बरे चरत् । प्रदीप्तदृग्भ्रुकुटितटोग्रदंष्ट्रकम् । ज्वलच्छिखं कटककिरीटकुण्डल । त्विषाद्भुतं हलधर ईषदत्रसत् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: nirīkṣya tadvapuralamambare caratpradīptadṛgbhrukuṭitaṭogradaṃṣṭrakamjvalacchikhaṃ kaṭakakirīṭakuṇḍalatviṣādbhutaṃ haladhara īṣadatrasat
translation: Balarāma (the wielḍer of a plough as a weapon) was a bit disturbed to see the demon’s body soaring with crashing speed in the sky, and with burning eyes, terrific (frowning) brows, terrible jaws and teeth, hair like flames of blazing fire, weirdly dazzling with the splendour of gold wristlets, crown and ear-rings.

devanagari: अथागतस्मृतिरभयो रिपुं बलो विहाय सार्थमिव हरन्तमात्मनः । रुषाहनच्छिरसि दृढेन मुष्टिना सुराधिपो गिरिमिव वज्ररंहसा ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: athāgatasmṛtirabhayo ripuṃ balo vihāya sārthamiva harantamātmanaḥruṣāhanacchirasi dṛḍhena muṣṭinā surādhipo girimiva vajraraṃhasā
translation: The very next moment, Balarāma regained his consciousness (of his divine nature) and grew fearless. And like Indra striking vehemently a mountain with his Vajra, in great rage, he dealt a heavy blow with his fist on the head of his enemy who was carrying him away from his group of cowherd friends.

devanagari: स आहतः सपदि विशीर्णमस्तको मुखाद्वमन्रुधिरमपस्मृतोऽसुरः । महारवं व्यसुरपतत्समीरयन्गिरिर्यथा मघवत आयुधाहतः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa āhataḥ sapadi viśīrṇamastako mukhādvamanrudhiramapasmṛto'suraḥmahāravaṃ vyasurapatatsamīrayangiriryathā maghavata āyudhāhataḥ
translation: The moment the blow descended on his head, it was shattered to pieces; vomitting blood profusely (from his mouth), he lost his consciousness. Giving out a terrific roar, the demon fell dead, like a mountain struck down by Indra’s weapon (thunderbolt).

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा प्रलम्बं निहतं बलेन बलशालिना । गोपाः सुविस्मिता आसन्साधु साध्विति वादिनः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā pralambaṃ nihataṃ balena balaśālināgopāḥ suvismitā āsansādhu sādhviti vādinaḥ
translation: Seeing the demon Pralamba killed by the mighty Balarāma, the cowherds were extremely astonished and shouted, “Bravo! well done!”.

devanagari: आशिषोऽभिगृणन्तस्तं प्रशशंसुस्तदर्हणम् । प्रेत्यागतमिवालिङ्ग्य प्रेमविह्वलचेतसः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: āśiṣo'bhigṛṇantastaṃ praśaśaṃsustadarhaṇampretyāgatamivāliṅgya premavihvalacetasaḥ
translation: Pronouncing benedictions on him, they highly praised him. And he deserved it. With their hearts overwhelmed with affection, they embraced him as one returned from the realm of death.

devanagari: पापे प्रलम्बे निहते देवाः परमनिर्वृताः । अभ्यवर्षन्बलं माल्यैः शशंसुः साधु साध्विति ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: pāpe pralambe nihate devāḥ paramanirvṛtāḥabhyavarṣanbalaṃ mālyaiḥ śaśaṃsuḥ sādhu sādhviti
translation:
When the sinful demon Pralamba was killed, gods felt highly gratified and happy. They showered on Balarāma wreaths of flowers and applauded him with their approbations “well done! Well done!”
[back to top]
